_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in performing format conversions to create a standard representation of the data.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in writing production-quality data transformation code in SQL and at least one general-purpose programming language (Python or Scala), including unit testing and documentation.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI TF domain. TF-S-001 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: TF-S-002 (TF, cosine: 0.511), OP-K-005 (OP, cosine: 0.488).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.0/10 (raw 0.597). Shared vocabulary: [data, skill]. DCWF-only terms [conversions, create, format, performing, representation, standard] vs WIDAI-only terms [code, documentation, general, language, least, one] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.874 (P=0.906, R=0.845). P exceeds R by 0.061 — the DCWF element's 13 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 24 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (100.0%). "Skill in performing format conversions to create a standard representation of th" and "Skill in writing production-quality data transformation code in SQL and at least" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.513 < STS 0.597 (gap -0.085). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [conversions, create, format] and [code, documentation, general] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 8.8%. Shared tokens: [data, skill]. DCWF-only: [conversions, create, format, performing, representation, standard]. WIDAI-only: [code, documentation, general, language, least, one].
